title: CrossDocumentLinks
description: Entities that appear in 2+ independently-extracted output/*.json documents, grouped by exact (normalized) name/mention match. Produced by link.py; not written by main.py.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when link.py was run.

source_output_dir.description: Directory that was scanned for ExtractionResult files (--output-dir).

documents.description: Every ExtractionResult file actually included in this run, in path-sorted order.

id.description: Output filename stem (e.g. 'foo' for output/foo.json). Used as the document identifier throughout this file.

source.description: The document's document.source value (local file path the extraction was run against).

skipped_files.description: output/ files not ingested (*.error.json, invalid JSON, wrong shape, or a duplicate document.source superseded by a newer extraction), and why.

ambiguous_local_matches.description: Normalized surface forms that collided between two DIFFERENT entities within the SAME document (e.g. two people both aliased the same way). Excluded from all cross-document matching rather than arbitrarily assigned to one entity.

document.description: Document id (see documents[].id) where the collision occurred.

surface_form.description: The normalized (NFC, casefolded, whitespace-collapsed) form that was ambiguous.

entities.description: Canonical names of the distinct entities that share this surface form.

groups.description: Shared-entity groups: each one real-world entity recurring across 2+ documents.

id.description: Stable within this file only, e.g. 'group-0001'.

representative_name.description: Deterministically chosen display name for the group: the longest member local_name, alphabetical tiebreak.

representative_type.description: The type shared by all members, or (if they disagree) the highest-priority type in PERSON/ORGANIZATION/LOCATION/PRODUCT/EVENT/OTHER order.

type_conflict.description: True if member documents disagree on this entity's type. Per-document types remain visible in `members` either way.

documents.description: Document ids (see documents[].id) spanned by this group, sorted.

members.description: One entry per document contributing to this group, with that document's own local view of the entity.

link_evidence.description: The specific surface-form matches that connected this group's documents. Groups are transitive (union-find), so with chained matches this list may not include every pair of member documents directly — it records the actual matches, not the full derived closure.

surface_form.description: Normalized (NFC, casefolded, whitespace-collapsed) form shared between the two documents.
